summary: Reviewed the setup-secret hardening, client polling fallbacks, host state reconciliation, and live-stage validation additions. The main architectural risk is game state now being written from multiple asynchronous sources without an ordering contract, after removing the previous monotonic guard. The stage validation changes are directionally sound but need verification against the existing pause/validation state machine.
appears_sound: `src/app/api/setup/route.ts:1` keeps setup-secret validation inside a server API route and uses fixed-length SHA-256 digests before `timingSafeEqual`, avoiding a direct variable-length secret comparison.

`src/app/display/[sessionId]/display-ui.tsx:138` and `src/app/player/[sessionId]/player-ui.tsx:165` add interval cleanup plus `visibilitychange` cleanup, which fits the realtime-plus-polling fallback pattern in the project guidance.
`src/app/host/actions.ts:1123` re-fetches live game/session/state server-side before recording a winner instead of trusting client-supplied stage or call-count values.

findings.description: Host game state now has multiple asynchronous writers, Realtime and polling, that assign whole snapshots without any ordering or version contract. This fixes legitimate void regressions only by removing stale-snapshot protection, so delayed heartbeat/realtime/poll responses can overwrite a newer called-number state during a live game.
findings.evidence: The previous guard was removed and both Realtime and polling now call `setCurrentGameState(payload.new)` / `setCurrentGameState(freshState)` directly at `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:391` and `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:433`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If Supabase delivery plus all polling responses are guaranteed to be ordered relative to every state mutation, the raw assignment would be safe, but the old code comment explicitly assumed stale heartbeat updates could arrive.
findings.what_would_confirm: Simulate call-number, heartbeat, void-last-number, and delayed poll/realtime delivery in different orders and verify the host UI never loses or resurrects called numbers.

findings.description: `recordWinner` and `announceWin` now reject any state other than `in_progress`, which may conflict with the existing claim-validation state machine if `pauseForValidation` changes status before the host records a winner. That would make the live validation path structurally unable to complete after a pause.
findings.evidence: `recordWinner` returns an error unless `liveStateRow.status === 'in_progress'` at `src/app/host/actions.ts:1139`, while the host UI imports `pauseForValidation`, `resumeGame`, `announceWin`, and `recordWinner` together at `src/app/host/[sessionId]/[gameId]/game-control.tsx:7`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The pause action may leave `game_states.status` as `in_progress` or the UI may record winners before pausing, but that contract is not visible in the pack.
findings.what_would_confirm: Trace the host claim flow and the `pauseForValidation` implementation to confirm the exact status when `announceWin` and `recordWinner` are called.

findings.description: The new player/display polling fallback duplicates the same session/game-state reconciliation logic in two client components instead of sharing a single ordered subscription-poll reducer. This creates drift risk because stage, active-game, prize-text, visibility, and cancellation behavior must now remain identical across both public views.
findings.evidence: Nearly identical polling effects were added in `src/app/player/[sessionId]/player-ui.tsx:165` and `src/app/display/[sessionId]/display-ui.tsx:138`, each directly querying `sessions` and `game_states_public` and setting local state.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: For tonightâ€™s scope, duplication may be an acceptable small blast-radius fix if both views were intentionally patched independently.
findings.what_would_confirm: Compare the full player and display flows after active-game changes, voids, stage advances, and hidden-tab recovery to see whether the two views stay in lockstep.

empty_categories: No server/client boundary violation found in the setup-secret change; `node:crypto` is imported only from an API route.
No new service-role client usage was introduced in client components in the pack.
No schema or migration coupling issues were introduced by this diff; no database schema files changed.
